preamble: The shift from the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] to a retroflex stop [ɖ] is a form of fronting and fortition. It occurs when a velar sound retracts its place of articulation and strengthens into a full stop, often under the influence of neighboring retroflex sounds.

examples.note: Development of retroflex series where velar sounds fronted and hardened.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
